items.title: What is AI
items.description: Artificial intelligence is the broader goal of machines performing intelligent tasks; machine learning is the dominant data-driven approach within AI today—learning patterns from examples rather than encoding all behavior as explicit rules.

items.title: Supervised learning
items.description: The most common ML paradigm: models learn input-to-label mappings from labeled training data. Spam detection and price prediction are canonical supervised problems introduced under the ML umbrella.

items.title: Training and inference
items.description: Every ML system separates a training phase (fit parameters from data) from an inference phase (apply the trained model to new inputs). This split is the operational backbone of production ML.

items.title: Maximum likelihood estimation
items.description: Many ML training objectives—cross-entropy, Gaussian MSE—are negative log-likelihood under a stated model. MLE explains why "minimize loss on training data" is a principled way to learn parameters from data.
